panels.description: min(audio, video) quality. <40 = degraded call. <75 = warning. Absent when no streams active.
panels.title: Call Quality Score (primary alert metric)

panels.description: Audio quality 0-100. Penalized by concealment (70%) and packet loss (30%). Absent during DTX silence.
panels.title: Audio Quality Score

panels.description: Video quality 0-100. FPS >= 5 scores 100 (health, not capability). Decode errors penalize.
panels.title: Video Quality Score

panels.description: NetEQ expand ops/sec = packet loss concealment. 0=perfect, >5=degraded, >10=poor. High values during DTX silence (speaker muted) are normal — cross-reference with packets/sec.
panels.title: Audio Concealment Rate (expand/sec)

panels.description: NetEQ delay manager's jitter estimate. VoIP thresholds: <30ms excellent, <75ms acceptable, >=75ms degraded. This is the real jitter metric (not buffer size).
panels.title: Network Jitter (target_delay_ms)

panels.description: Proxy for packet loss (expand_per_sec / packets_per_sec). Includes late arrivals and jitter underruns. Transient spikes after DTX→active transition are normal.
panels.title: Audio Packet Loss %

panels.description: Audio RTP packets received/sec. Near 0 = speaker is silent (DTX). Use this to distinguish real packet loss from DTX silence in concealment panels.
panels.title: Audio Packets/sec (DTX indicator)

panels.description: Video bitrate observed by the receiver. Low bitrate + normal FPS = low-complexity scene. Low bitrate + low FPS = possible bandwidth constraint.
panels.title: Video Bitrate (kbps)

panels.description: Client-measured RTT to the relay server. If most participants spike simultaneously = server/upstream issue. Single participant spike = their network.
panels.title: Server RTT (ms)

panels.description: Count of participants with call_quality_score < 40 (degraded). When most participants degrade simultaneously, it indicates a server-side or upstream network issue.
panels.title: Participants with Degraded Audio (Call Score < 40)

panels.description: Browser tab visibility. Backgrounded tabs throttle JS timers to 1000ms, starving the audio decoder and freezing diagnostics. Ignore degraded metrics from hidden tabs.
panels.title: Tab Visibility (1=visible, 0=backgrounded)

panels.description: JS heap memory used (Chrome only). High memory may indicate a leak or too many decoded video streams.
panels.title: Client Memory Usage (MB)
